wikidata_candidates.reasoning: The two records describe completely different people. The first is an American-based plastics engineer (M.J. Savla) living in New Jersey, while the second is a Latvian biathlete (Linda Savİaka) born in 1984 who competed in the Winter Olympics. There is no overlap in name, location, profession, or nationality.

results.possibleIdentity: Manilal J. Savla (often referred to as M.J. Savla) was a plastics engineer and technical consultant who was active in the late 1960s and 1970s. He was primarily associated with Skeist Laboratories, Inc., a consulting firm specializing in polymers, adhesives, and coatings, which operated out of Hoboken and later Livingston, New Jersey. Savla was a significant contributor to the technical literature of the plastics and adhesives industry, most notably co-authoring chapters on "Epoxy Resins" and "Amino Resin Adhesives" in the second edition of the "Handbook of Adhesives" (1977), edited by Irving Skeist. He also co-authored the book "Epoxy Resins" (1977) as part of the "High Polymers" series. Savla held several patents assigned to Skeist Laboratories, often collaborating with Irving Skeist and Jerry Miron. His technical expertise spanned chemical engineering, adhesives, plastics, and polymers. He was also a member of the Society of Plastics Engineers (SPE) and is documented in the technical directory of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization that paired artists with engineers to foster innovative collaborations.
